description: The expression `remote.repo_pr_loaded.get(agent_repo) is True if agent_repo else False` is parsed by Python as `remote.repo_pr_loaded.get(agent_repo) is (True if agent_repo else False)` due to ternary having lower precedence than `is`. The intended logic is `(remote.repo_pr_loaded.get(agent_repo) is True) if agent_repo else False`. Both parsings coincidentally yield the same result in practice (when agent_repo is None, .get(None) returns None, and `None is False` is False), but the code is misleading and fragile. Explicit parentheses are needed.

Same ambiguous precedence issue as line 142: `remote.repo_pr_loaded.get(agent_repo) is True if agent_repo else False` is parsed as `is (True if agent_repo else False)` rather than the intended `(is True) if agent_repo else False`.
